Ingredients:
• 5 lbs of corn mash (you can use a mix of corn, rye, and barley)
• 2 lbs of sugar
• 1 lb of yeast (such as champagne or brewer's yeast)
• 1 lb of water
• A distillation apparatus (still) with a condenser and collection vessel
Instructions:

2. Once the mash is cooled, add the yeast and let it ferment for 3-5 days, or until the mixture has stopped bubbling.
3. Transfer the fermented mash to the still and heat it to around 180°F (82°C) to separate the liquid from the solids.

5. Disable the “Allow users to connect remotely by using Remote Desktop Services” policy.

1. Open PowerShell as an administrator.
2. Use the following command to disable the user’s computer: `Stop-Computer -ComputerName <ComputerName> -Force`
3. Replace `<ComputerName>` with the actual name of the computer you want to disable.
